originalTitle: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
title: A Story About Rules and Being Safe!
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, bringing you important stories from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we have a story from a big, busy city called Johannesburg in South Africa. It's a story that helps us remember why rules are so important, especially when it comes to being safe. We are going to talk about a young man, and something that happened with a special kind of object.

A Young Man and a Serious Mistake

There is a young man named Bellarmine Mugabe. He is a grown-up, not a child like you. This story is about something he did. One day, at his house in Johannesburg, something not-so-good happened. Bellarmine pointed a gun. A gun is a very serious thing. It is not a toy. It is a tool that can be very, very dangerous if not used correctly. Pointing a gun at someone is a very big mistake. It can make people very scared, and it can cause real harm. *Did you know? Johannesburg is one of the biggest cities in South Africa! It's famous for its gold and busy streets.*

What is a Gun, Really?

Let's talk a little more about guns. Guns are made for special purposes, like helping police officers keep us safe, or for hunting in certain places. But they are *never* for playing with. They are very powerful. That's why grown-ups who have guns must be super careful. They must keep them locked away, far from children, and only use them when it's absolutely necessary and safe. This young man, Bellarmine, pointed a gun, and that is against the rules. It made someone feel very unsafe. *Did you know? In many places, it is against the law to even touch a gun unless you are a trained adult!*

What Does 'Guilty' Mean?

When something like this happens, people go to a special place called a court. A court is like a big classroom where grown-ups talk about rules and what happens when someone breaks a rule. In court, Bellarmine said that he was "guilty" of pointing the gun. What does "guilty" mean? It means he said, "Yes, I did it. I made that mistake." It's like when you tell your teacher, "Yes, I was the one who accidentally spilled the paint." Saying "guilty" means you admit what you did. It's an important step in taking responsibility for your actions. *Did you know? Courts are very old places! They help make sure everyone in a community is treated fairly and follows the rules.*

Why Do We Have Rules?

We have rules for a very important reason: to keep everyone safe and happy! Think about it. At school, you have rules like "don't run in the hallways" so no one trips. At home, you have rules like "don't touch the hot stove" to keep you from getting burned. Rules about dangerous things like guns are some of the most important rules of all. They are there to protect people from getting hurt, either by accident or on purpose. When Bellarmine pointed the gun, he broke a very important rule about safety. *Did you know? Rules are like invisible helpers! They guide us to do things that are good for ourselves and for others.*

Learning From Mistakes

Everyone makes mistakes sometimes, even grown-ups. The important thing is what happens after the mistake. When Bellarmine said he was guilty, it meant he was ready to face what happens next. The court will now decide what Bellarmine needs to do to learn from his mistake and make things right. Maybe he will have to pay money, or maybe he will have to do special work to help his community. Whatever it is, it's a way for him to understand the serious nature of what he did. It shows that breaking important safety rules has consequences. *Did you know? Sometimes, when grown-ups make mistakes, they have to do "community service," which means helping out in their town to make things better!*

Always Be Safe and Follow Rules!

This story from South Africa reminds all of us, young and old, how important it is to follow rules. Especially rules about things that can be dangerous. If you ever see something that looks like a gun, or any dangerous object, what should you do? Tell a trusted grown-up right away! Do not touch it. Do not play with it. Always remember to ask for help if you see something that makes you feel unsafe or worried. Our communities are safer when everyone understands and follows the rules. Let's all work together to make our homes and our neighborhoods the safest places they can be! *Did you know? There are special programs to teach grown-ups and kids about gun safety, so everyone knows how to be careful!*
summary: A young man in South Africa named Bellarmine Mugabe said he was guilty of pointing a gun, which is a big mistake and reminds us all about how important it is to follow safety rules.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us that rules keep everyone safe, especially rules about dangerous things like guns, and that grown-ups also need to be careful and learn from their mistakes.

originalTitle: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
content: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

content: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

originalTitle: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
content: Brent crude sinks 10% after Iran says the key waterway is completely open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
content: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

originalTitle: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
content: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

originalTitle: Guardiola says he enjoys watching Arsenal play
title: Guardiola says he enjoys watching Arsenal play
content: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says he enjoys watching Arsenal play as he can "learn a lot" from them.

originalTitle: Italy made me a manager when England 'discouraged' me - Cole
title: Italy made me a manager when England 'discouraged' me - Cole
content: Ashley Cole won 107 caps for England, seventh on the all‑time list, but says he was "discouraged" by those in the football pyramid from becoming a head coach.

originalTitle: Leicester need something special to beat drop - Rowett
title: Leicester need something special to beat drop - Rowett
content: Leicester City boss Gary Rowett says "belief" in increasingly dire times will be key to the Foxes' Championship survival hopes.
